    "R1.2": "The delegation is exclusively logical-consistency-focused. Multiple inference chains must be checked: (1) self-reports treated as ground truth — the model is trained and evaluated on labels whose validity is assumed but not established, creating a circularity risk; (2) the deployment scope paragraph claims SUMSCREEN requires no self-report labels at inference time, yet the entire training and evaluation pipeline is built on self-reports — these two claims sit in tension; (3) the conclusion paragraph conflates the detector's AUC with a calibrated prevalence estimate, then uses the training-set prevalence (41%) as external validation of the detector's output — this is circular: the prevalence the model learned from is the same prevalence used to validate it; (4) the comparison to 'prior MTurk summarization studies' is asserted without specifying what those studies measured or whether their conditions are comparable.",
    "R1.3": "The AUC-to-prevalence inference in the Results section is the sharpest logical fault and is directly tied to how results are reported: the artifact moves from a discrimination metric (AUC 0.93) to a calibration claim ('calibrated prevalence estimator') with no intermediate evidence — no calibration curve, no precision-recall analysis at the deployment threshold, and no out-of-sample prevalence test."
  },

rationale.R1.2: The delegation is exclusively logical-consistency-focused. Multiple inference chains must be checked: (1) self-reports treated as ground truth — the model is trained and evaluated on labels whose validity is assumed but not established, creating a circularity risk; (2) the deployment scope paragraph claims SUMSCREEN requires no self-report labels at inference time, yet the entire training and evaluation pipeline is built on self-reports — these two claims sit in tension; (3) the conclusion paragraph conflates the detector's AUC with a calibrated prevalence estimate, then uses the training-set prevalence (41%) as external validation of the detector's output — this is circular: the prevalence the model learned from is the same prevalence used to validate it; (4) the comparison to 'prior MTurk summarization studies' is asserted without specifying what those studies measured or whether their conditions are comparable.
rationale.R1.3: The AUC-to-prevalence inference in the Results section is the sharpest logical fault and is directly tied to how results are reported: the artifact moves from a discrimination metric (AUC 0.93) to a calibration claim ('calibrated prevalence estimator') with no intermediate evidence — no calibration curve, no precision-recall analysis at the deployment threshold, and no out-of-sample prevalence test.
